sEn: YOLOv3 is a fast and accurate object detector, achieving 57.9 mAP@50 in 51 ms on a Titan X, 3.8x faster than RetinaNet with similar performance.
sZh: YOLOv3 是一个快速且准确的目标检测器，在 Titan X 上以 51 毫秒达到 57.9 mAP@50，性能与 RetinaNet 相当但速度快 3.8 倍。
absEn: We present some updates to YOLO! We made a bunch of little design changes to make it better. We also trained this new network that's pretty swell. It's a little bigger than last time but more accurate. It's still fast though, don't worry. At 320x320 YOLOv3 runs in 22 ms at 28.2 mAP, as accurate as SSD but three times faster. When we look at the old .5 IOU mAP detection metric YOLOv3 is quite good. It achieves 57.9 mAP@50 in 51 ms on a Titan X, compared to 57.5 mAP@50 in 198 ms by RetinaNet, similar performance but 3.8x faster. As always, all the code is online at https://pjreddie.com/yolo/
absZh: 我们介绍了 YOLO 的一些更新！我们进行了一系列小的设计改进以提升性能。我们还训练了一个新的网络，效果相当不错。它比之前的版本稍大，但更准确。不过它仍然很快，别担心。在 320x320 分辨率下，YOLOv3 运行时间为 22 毫秒，mAP 为 28.2，与 SSD 精度相当但速度快三倍。当使用旧的 .5 IOU mAP 检测指标时，YOLOv3 表现相当出色。它在 Titan X 上以 51 毫秒达到 57.9 mAP@50，而 RetinaNet 需要 198 毫秒才能达到 57.5 mAP@50，性能相似但速度快 3.8 倍。一如既往，所有代码均可在 https://pjreddie.com/yolo/ 获取。

contrib.en: Proposes YOLOv3, an incremental improvement to YOLO with better accuracy and speed.
contrib.zh: 提出YOLOv3，对YOLO进行增量改进，提升精度和速度。

contrib.en: Introduces Darknet-53, a new feature extractor balancing efficiency and performance.
contrib.zh: 引入Darknet-53，一种平衡效率与性能的新特征提取器。

contrib.en: Adopts multi-scale predictions using feature pyramid networks for improved small object detection.
contrib.zh: 采用特征金字塔网络的多尺度预测，改进小目标检测。

contrib.en: Uses independent logistic classifiers for multilabel classification, avoiding softmax limitations.
contrib.zh: 使用独立逻辑分类器进行多标签分类，避免softmax限制。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art speed-accuracy trade-off on COCO, e.g., 57.9 mAP@50 in 51 ms.
contrib.zh: 在COCO上实现速度与精度的最优权衡，例如51毫秒内达到57.9 mAP@50。

contrib.en: Open-sources code and model for reproducibility.
contrib.zh: 开源代码和模型以促进可复现性。

limits.en: Performance drops significantly at higher IOU thresholds (e.g., 0.75), indicating poor box alignment.
limits.zh: 在高IOU阈值（如0.75）下性能显著下降，表明边界框对齐不佳。

limits.en: Underperforms on medium and large objects compared to small objects in COCO.
limits.zh: 在COCO中，中大型目标的性能不如小型目标。

limits.en: Does not achieve top results on COCO average AP (0.5:0.95), lagging behind RetinaNet.
limits.zh: 在COCO平均AP（0.5:0.95）上未达到最佳结果，落后于RetinaNet。

limits.en: Some attempted improvements (e.g., focal loss, dual IOU thresholds) did not yield gains.
limits.zh: 部分尝试的改进（如焦点损失、双IOU阈值）未带来收益。

items.en: We present some updates to YOLO! We made a bunch of little design changes to make it better. We also trained this new network that’s pretty swell. It’s a little bigger than last time but more accurate. It’s still fast though, don’t worry. At 320×320320320320\times 320 YOLOv3 runs in 22 ms at 28.2 mAP, as accurate as SSD but three times faster. When we look at the old .5 IOU mAP detection metric YOLOv3 is quite good. It achieves 57.957.957.9 AP50 in 51 ms on a Titan X, compared to 57.557.557.5 AP50 in 198 ms by RetinaNet, similar performance but 3.8×\times faster. As always, all the code is online at https://pjreddie.com/yolo/.
items.zh: 我们介绍YOLO的一些更新！我们做了一堆小的设计改进来提升性能。我们还训练了一个相当不错的新网络。它比上次稍大一些，但更准确。不过它仍然很快，别担心。在320×320分辨率下，YOLOv3运行时间为22毫秒，mAP为28.2，与SSD精度相当但速度快三倍。当我们查看旧的0.5 IOU mAP检测指标时，YOLOv3表现相当好。它在Titan X上以51毫秒达到57.9 AP50，而RetinaNet在198毫秒内达到57.5 AP50，性能相似但速度快3.8倍。一如既往，所有代码都在线：https://pjreddie.com/yolo/。

items.en: Sometimes you just kinda phone it in for a year, you know? I didn’t do a whole lot of research this year. Spent a lot of time on Twitter. Played around with GANs a little. I had a little momentum left over from last year [12] [1]; I managed to make some improvements to YOLO. But, honestly, nothing like super interesting, just a bunch of small changes that make it better. I also helped out with other people’s research a little.
items.zh: 有时候你一年里只是敷衍了事，你知道吗？我今年没做太多研究。花了很多时间在Twitter上。稍微玩了一下GAN。去年留下了一点动力[12][1]；我设法对YOLO做了一些改进。但说实话，没什么特别有趣的，只是一堆小改进让它变得更好。我还帮别人做了一点研究。

items.en: Actually, that’s what brings us here today. We have a camera-ready deadline [4] and we need to cite some of the random updates I made to YOLO but we don’t have a source. So get ready for a TECH REPORT!
items.zh: 实际上，这正是我们今天在这里的原因。我们有一个截稿日期[4]，需要引用我对YOLO做的一些随机更新，但我们没有来源。所以准备好迎接一份技术报告吧！

items.en: The great thing about tech reports is that they don’t need intros, y’all know why we’re here. So the end of this introduction will signpost for the rest of the paper. First we’ll tell you what the deal is with YOLOv3. Then we’ll tell you how we do. We’ll also tell you about some things we tried that didn’t work. Finally we’ll contemplate what this all means.
items.zh: 技术报告的好处是它们不需要引言，大家都知道我们为什么在这里。所以这篇引言的结尾将为论文的其余部分做指引。首先，我们会告诉你YOLOv3是怎么回事。然后我们会告诉你我们的表现如何。我们还会告诉你一些我们尝试过但没成功的事情。最后，我们会思考这一切意味着什么。

items.en: So here’s the deal with YOLOv3: We mostly took good ideas from other people. We also trained a new classifier network that’s better than the other ones. We’ll just take you through the whole system from scratch so you can understand it all.
items.zh: 所以YOLOv3的情况是这样的：我们主要借鉴了别人的好想法。我们还训练了一个新的分类器网络，比其他网络更好。我们将从头带你了解整个系统，以便你完全理解。

items.capEn: Figure 1: We adapt this figure from the Focal Loss paper [9]. YOLOv3 runs significantly faster than other detection methods with comparable performance. Times from either an M40 or Titan X, they are basically the same GPU.
items.capZh: 图1：我们改编自Focal Loss论文[9]的这张图。YOLOv3在性能相当的情况下运行速度明显快于其他检测方法。时间来自M40或Titan X，它们基本上是相同的GPU。

items.en: Following YOLO9000 our system predicts bounding boxes using dimension clusters as anchor boxes [15]. The network predicts 4 coordinates for each bounding box, txsubscript𝑡𝑥t_{x}, tysubscript𝑡𝑦t_{y}, twsubscript𝑡𝑤t_{w}, thsubscript𝑡ℎt_{h}. If the cell is offset from the top left corner of the image by (cx,cy)subscript𝑐𝑥subscript𝑐𝑦(c_{x},c_{y}) and the bounding box prior has width and height pwsubscript𝑝𝑤p_{w}, phsubscript𝑝ℎp_{h}, then the predictions correspond to:
items.zh: 继YOLO9000之后，我们的系统使用维度聚类作为锚框来预测边界框[15]。网络为每个边界框预测4个坐标：t_x, t_y, t_w, t_h。如果单元格相对于图像左上角的偏移为(c_x, c_y)，且边界框先验的宽度和高度为p_w, p_h，则预测对应为：

items.en: During training we use sum of squared error loss. If the ground truth for some coordinate prediction is t^*subscript^𝑡*\hat{t}_{\mbox{*}} our gradient is the ground truth value (computed from the ground truth box) minus our prediction: t^*−t*subscript^𝑡*subscript𝑡*\hat{t}_{\mbox{*}}-t_{\mbox{*}}. This ground truth value can be easily computed by inverting the equations above.
items.zh: 在训练过程中，我们使用平方和误差损失。如果某个坐标预测的真实值为t̂_*，我们的梯度就是真实值（从真实框计算得出）减去我们的预测：t̂_* - t_*。这个真实值可以通过反转上述方程轻松计算。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Bounding boxes with dimension priors and location prediction. We predict the width and height of the box as offsets from cluster centroids. We predict the center coordinates of the box relative to the location of filter application using a sigmoid function. This figure blatantly self-plagiarized from [15].
items.capZh: 图2：具有维度先验和位置预测的边界框。我们预测框的宽度和高度作为聚类中心的偏移。我们使用sigmoid函数预测框的中心坐标相对于滤波器应用位置。这张图明显是从[15]自我剽窃的。

items.en: YOLOv3 predicts an objectness score for each bounding box using logistic regression. This should be 1 if the bounding box prior overlaps a ground truth object by more than any other bounding box prior. If the bounding box prior is not the best but does overlap a ground truth object by more than some threshold we ignore the prediction, following [17]. We use the threshold of .5.5.5. Unlike [17] our system only assigns one bounding box prior for each ground truth object. If a bounding box prior is not assigned to a ground truth object it incurs no loss for coordinate or class predictions, only objectness.
items.zh: YOLOv3使用逻辑回归为每个边界框预测一个物体性分数。如果边界框先验与真实物体的重叠超过任何其他边界框先验，则该分数应为1。如果边界框先验不是最好的，但与真实物体的重叠超过某个阈值，我们忽略该预测，遵循[17]。我们使用阈值0.5。与[17]不同，我们的系统只为每个真实物体分配一个边界框先验。如果边界框先验未分配给真实物体，则坐标或类别预测不产生损失，只有物体性损失。

items.en: Each box predicts the classes the bounding box may contain using multilabel classification. We do not use a softmax as we have found it is unnecessary for good performance, instead we simply use independent logistic classifiers. During training we use binary cross-entropy loss for the class predictions.
items.zh: 每个框使用多标签分类预测边界框可能包含的类别。我们不使用softmax，因为我们发现它对良好性能不是必需的，而是简单地使用独立的逻辑分类器。在训练过程中，我们对类别预测使用二元交叉熵损失。

items.en: This formulation helps when we move to more complex domains like the Open Images Dataset [7]. In this dataset there are many overlapping labels (i.e. Woman and Person). Using a softmax imposes the assumption that each box has exactly one class which is often not the case. A multilabel approach better models the data.
items.zh: 这种公式有助于我们迁移到更复杂的领域，如Open Images数据集[7]。在这个数据集中，有许多重叠的标签（例如，女人和人）。使用softmax会强加每个框只有一个类别的假设，但通常情况并非如此。多标签方法更好地建模了数据。

items.en: YOLOv3 predicts boxes at 3 different scales. Our system extracts features from those scales using a similar concept to feature pyramid networks [8]. From our base feature extractor we add several convolutional layers. The last of these predicts a 3-d tensor encoding bounding box, objectness, and class predictions. In our experiments with COCO [10] we predict 3 boxes at each scale so the tensor is N×N×[3∗(4+1+80)]𝑁𝑁delimited-[]34180N\times N\times[3*(4+1+80)] for the 4 bounding box offsets, 1 objectness prediction, and 80 class predictions.
items.zh: YOLOv3在3个不同尺度上预测框。我们的系统使用类似于特征金字塔网络[8]的概念从这些尺度提取特征。从我们的基础特征提取器开始，我们添加了几个卷积层。最后一层预测一个3D张量，编码边界框、物体性和类别预测。在我们使用COCO[10]的实验中，我们在每个尺度预测3个框，因此张量为N×N×[3*(4+1+80)]，对应4个边界框偏移、1个物体性预测和80个类别预测。

items.en: Next we take the feature map from 2 layers previous and upsample it by 2×2\times. We also take a feature map from earlier in the network and merge it with our upsampled features using concatenation. This method allows us to get more meaningful semantic information from the upsampled features and finer-grained information from the earlier feature map. We then add a few more convolutional layers to process this combined feature map, and eventually predict a similar tensor, although now twice the size.
items.zh: 接下来，我们取前两层的特征图，并将其上采样2倍。我们还从网络早期取一个特征图，并通过拼接与上采样后的特征合并。这种方法使我们能够从上采样特征中获得更有意义的语义信息，并从早期特征图中获得更细粒度的信息。然后我们添加几个卷积层来处理这个合并后的特征图，并最终预测一个类似的张量，尽管现在尺寸是原来的两倍。

items.en: We perform the same design one more time to predict boxes for the final scale. Thus our predictions for the 3rd scale benefit from all the prior computation as well as fine-grained features from early on in the network.
items.zh: 我们再次执行相同的设计，以预测最终尺度的框。因此，我们对第三尺度的预测受益于所有先前的计算以及网络早期的细粒度特征。

items.en: We still use k-means clustering to determine our bounding box priors. We just sort of chose 9 clusters and 3 scales arbitrarily and then divide up the clusters evenly across scales. On the COCO dataset the 9 clusters were: (10×13),(16×30),(33×23),(30×61),(62×45),(59×119),(116×90),(156×198),(373×326)101316303323306162455911911690156198373326(10\times 13),(16\times 30),(33\times 23),(30\times 61),(62\times 45),(59\times 119),(116\times 90),(156\times 198),(373\times 326).
items.zh: 我们仍然使用k-means聚类来确定边界框先验。我们只是随意选择了9个聚类和3个尺度，然后将聚类均匀分配到各个尺度。在COCO数据集上，9个聚类是：(10×13), (16×30), (33×23), (30×61), (62×45), (59×119), (116×90), (156×198), (373×326)。

items.en: We use a new network for performing feature extraction. Our new network is a hybrid approach between the network used in YOLOv2, Darknet-19, and that newfangled residual network stuff. Our network uses successive 3×3333\times 3 and 1×1111\times 1 convolutional layers but now has some shortcut connections as well and is significantly larger. It has 53 convolutional layers so we call it…. wait for it….. Darknet-53!
items.zh: 我们使用一个新的网络进行特征提取。我们的新网络是YOLOv2中使用的网络Darknet-19与那种新奇的残差网络之间的混合方法。我们的网络使用连续的3×3和1×1卷积层，但现在也有一些快捷连接，并且规模明显更大。它有53个卷积层，所以我们称之为……等等……Darknet-53！

items.en: This new network is much more powerful than Darknet-19 but still more efficient than ResNet-101 or ResNet-152. Here are some ImageNet results:
items.zh: 这个新网络比Darknet-19强大得多，但仍然比ResNet-101或ResNet-152更高效。以下是一些ImageNet结果：

items.en: Each network is trained with identical settings and tested at 256×256256256256\times 256, single crop accuracy. Run times are measured on a Titan X at 256×256256256256\times 256. Thus Darknet-53 performs on par with state-of-the-art classifiers but with fewer floating point operations and more speed. Darknet-53 is better than ResNet-101 and 1.5×1.5\times faster. Darknet-53 has similar performance to ResNet-152 and is 2×2\times faster.
items.zh: 每个网络在相同设置下训练，并在256×256分辨率下测试，单次裁剪精度。运行时间在Titan X上以256×256测量。因此，Darknet-53的性能与最先进的分类器相当，但浮点运算更少，速度更快。Darknet-53优于ResNet-101，速度快1.5倍。Darknet-53与ResNet-152性能相似，速度快2倍。

items.en: Darknet-53 also achieves the highest measured floating point operations per second. This means the network structure better utilizes the GPU, making it more efficient to evaluate and thus faster. That’s mostly because ResNets have just way too many layers and aren’t very efficient.
items.zh: Darknet-53还实现了最高的测量浮点运算每秒。这意味着网络结构更好地利用了GPU，使其评估更高效，因此更快。这主要是因为ResNet的层数太多，效率不高。

items.en: We still train on full images with no hard negative mining or any of that stuff. We use multi-scale training, lots of data augmentation, batch normalization, all the standard stuff. We use the Darknet neural network framework for training and testing [14].
items.zh: 我们仍然在完整图像上训练，没有使用难例挖掘或类似技术。我们采用多尺度训练、大量数据增强、批量归一化等标准方法。我们使用Darknet神经网络框架进行训练和测试[14]。

items.en: YOLOv3 is pretty good! See table 3. In terms of COCOs weird average mean AP metric it is on par with the SSD variants but is 3×3\times faster. It is still quite a bit behind other models like RetinaNet in this metric though.
items.zh: YOLOv3相当不错！见表3。在COCO奇怪的均值平均AP指标上，它与SSD变体相当，但速度快3倍。不过，在此指标上它仍落后于RetinaNet等其他模型。

items.en: However, when we look at the “old” detection metric of mAP at IOU=.5absent.5=.5 (or AP50 in the chart) YOLOv3 is very strong. It is almost on par with RetinaNet and far above the SSD variants. This indicates that YOLOv3 is a very strong detector that excels at producing decent boxes for objects. However, performance drops significantly as the IOU threshold increases indicating YOLOv3 struggles to get the boxes perfectly aligned with the object.
items.zh: 然而，当我们查看“旧”检测指标——IOU=0.5时的mAP（图表中的AP50）时，YOLOv3非常强大。它几乎与RetinaNet持平，并远超SSD变体。这表明YOLOv3是一个非常强大的检测器，擅长生成合适的物体框。但随着IOU阈值提高，性能显著下降，表明YOLOv3难以使框与物体完美对齐。

items.en: In the past YOLO struggled with small objects. However, now we see a reversal in that trend. With the new multi-scale predictions we see YOLOv3 has relatively high APS performance. However, it has comparatively worse performance on medium and larger size objects. More investigation is needed to get to the bottom of this.
items.zh: 过去YOLO难以处理小物体。但现在我们看到这一趋势逆转。借助新的多尺度预测，YOLOv3的APS性能相对较高。然而，在中型和大型物体上性能相对较差。需要进一步研究以弄清原因。

items.en: When we plot accuracy vs speed on the AP50 metric (see figure 5) we see YOLOv3 has significant benefits over other detection systems. Namely, it’s faster and better.
items.zh: 当我们在AP50指标上绘制精度与速度的关系图（见图5）时，我们看到YOLOv3相比其他检测系统具有显著优势。即，它更快且更好。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Again adapted from the [9], this time displaying speed/accuracy tradeoff on the mAP at .5 IOU metric. You can tell YOLOv3 is good because it’s very high and far to the left. Can you cite your own paper? Guess who’s going to try, this guy →→\rightarrow [16]. Oh, I forgot, we also fix a data loading bug in YOLOv2, that helped by like 2 mAP. Just sneaking this in here to not throw off layout.
items.capZh: 图3：同样改编自[9]，这次展示在IOU=0.5的mAP指标上的速度/精度权衡。可以看出YOLOv3很好，因为它位置很高且靠左。你能引用自己的论文吗？猜猜谁会尝试？这家伙→[16]。哦，我忘了，我们还修复了YOLOv2中的一个数据加载错误，这帮助提升了约2个mAP。只是顺便提一下，以免打乱布局。

full.sec: 4 Things We Tried That Didn’t Work

items.en: We tried lots of stuff while we were working on YOLOv3. A lot of it didn’t work. Here’s the stuff we can remember.
items.zh: 在开发YOLOv3时，我们尝试了很多东西。很多都不奏效。以下是我们记得的内容。

items.en: Anchor box x,y𝑥𝑦x,y offset predictions. We tried using the normal anchor box prediction mechanism where you predict the x,y𝑥𝑦x,y offset as a multiple of the box width or height using a linear activation. We found this formulation decreased model stability and didn’t work very well.
items.zh: 锚框x,y偏移预测。我们尝试了常规的锚框预测机制，即使用线性激活将x,y偏移预测为框宽或高的倍数。我们发现这种公式降低了模型稳定性，效果不佳。

items.en: Linear x,y𝑥𝑦x,y predictions instead of logistic. We tried using a linear activation to directly predict the x,y𝑥𝑦x,y offset instead of the logistic activation. This led to a couple point drop in mAP.
items.zh: 线性x,y预测而非逻辑预测。我们尝试使用线性激活直接预测x,y偏移，而不是逻辑激活。这导致mAP下降了几个点。

items.en: Focal loss. We tried using focal loss. It dropped our mAP about 2 points. YOLOv3 may already be robust to the problem focal loss is trying to solve because it has separate objectness predictions and conditional class predictions. Thus for most examples there is no loss from the class predictions? Or something? We aren’t totally sure.
items.zh: Focal loss。我们尝试使用focal loss。它使我们的mAP下降了约2个点。YOLOv3可能已经对focal loss试图解决的问题具有鲁棒性，因为它有独立的物体性预测和条件类别预测。因此，对于大多数样本，类别预测没有损失？或者类似原因？我们不完全确定。

items.en: Dual IOU thresholds and truth assignment. Faster R-CNN uses two IOU thresholds during training. If a prediction overlaps the ground truth by .7 it is as a positive example, by [.3−.7]delimited-[].3.7[.3-.7] it is ignored, less than .3 for all ground truth objects it is a negative example. We tried a similar strategy but couldn’t get good results.
items.zh: 双IOU阈值和真值分配。Faster R-CNN在训练中使用两个IOU阈值。如果预测与真值的重叠为0.7，则视为正样本；在[0.3-0.7]之间则忽略；对所有真值物体小于0.3则为负样本。我们尝试了类似策略，但未能获得良好结果。

items.en: We quite like our current formulation, it seems to be at a local optima at least. It is possible that some of these techniques could eventually produce good results, perhaps they just need some tuning to stabilize the training.
items.zh: 我们很喜欢当前的公式，它至少似乎处于局部最优。这些技术中的一些可能最终能产生良好结果，也许只需要一些调整来稳定训练。

items.en: YOLOv3 is a good detector. It’s fast, it’s accurate. It’s not as great on the COCO average AP between .5 and .95 IOU metric. But it’s very good on the old detection metric of .5 IOU.
items.zh: YOLOv3是一个好的检测器。它快速、准确。在COCO的0.5到0.95 IOU平均AP指标上表现不佳，但在0.5 IOU的旧检测指标上非常好。

items.en: Why did we switch metrics anyway? The original COCO paper just has this cryptic sentence: “A full discussion of evaluation metrics will be added once the evaluation server is complete”. Russakovsky et al report that that humans have a hard time distinguishing an IOU of .3 from .5! “Training humans to visually inspect a bounding box with IOU of 0.3 and distinguish it from one with IOU 0.5 is surprisingly difficult.” [18] If humans have a hard time telling the difference, how much does it matter?
items.zh: 我们为什么切换指标？原始的COCO论文只有一句神秘的话：“评估服务器的完整讨论将在完成后添加”。Russakovsky等人报告说，人类很难区分IOU为0.3和0.5！“训练人类目视检查IOU为0.3的边界框并区分它与IOU为0.5的边界框是出奇困难的。”[18] 如果人类都难以区分，那这又有多大意义呢？

items.en: But maybe a better question is: “What are we going to do with these detectors now that we have them?” A lot of the people doing this research are at Google and Facebook. I guess at least we know the technology is in good hands and definitely won’t be used to harvest your personal information and sell it to…. wait, you’re saying that’s exactly what it will be used for?? Oh.
items.zh: 但也许更好的问题是：“现在我们有了这些检测器，我们打算用它们做什么？”很多从事这项研究的人在谷歌和Facebook工作。我想至少我们知道这项技术掌握在好人手中，绝对不会被用来收集你的个人信息并出售给……等等，你是说这正是它的用途？？哦。

items.en: Well the other people heavily funding vision research are the military and they’ve never done anything horrible like killing lots of people with new technology oh wait…..111The author is funded by the Office of Naval Research and Google.
items.zh: 另外，大量资助视觉研究的是军方，他们从未做过像用新技术杀死很多人这样可怕的事情，哦等等……1作者受海军研究办公室和谷歌资助。

items.en: I have a lot of hope that most of the people using computer vision are just doing happy, good stuff with it, like counting the number of zebras in a national park [13], or tracking their cat as it wanders around their house [19]. But computer vision is already being put to questionable use and as researchers we have a responsibility to at least consider the harm our work might be doing and think of ways to mitigate it. We owe the world that much.
items.zh: 我抱有很大希望，大多数使用计算机视觉的人只是在做快乐、有益的事情，比如数国家公园里的斑马数量[13]，或者跟踪他们的猫在家里闲逛[19]。但计算机视觉已经被用于可疑用途，作为研究人员，我们有责任至少考虑我们的工作可能造成的危害，并思考减轻危害的方法。我们欠世界这么多。

items.en: In closing, do not @ me. (Because I finally quit Twitter).
items.zh: 最后，不要@我。（因为我终于退出了Twitter。）
